title: Land local-symphony Phase 0 Go/eino bump
description: Before local-symphony consumes eino-tools wrappers, land a separate local-symphony PR that bumps go.mod from Go 1.25.0 to 1.25.5 and github.com/cloudwego/eino from v0.7.13 to v0.8.13, runs go mod tidy, updates any tool ABI shims needed by v0.8.x, and passes the local-symphony worker/tool/runtime test set. This must be complete before the deletion PR that replaces internal/worker/tools with thin eino-tools wrappers; do not bundle the baseline bump into that deletion PR.
notes:
Checked /home/infra-admin/git/local-symphony on 2026-05-25. Cannot safely land the separate Phase 0 bump from this session: checkout is on feat/react-agent-rewire with existing uncommitted user changes in go.mod and internal/tracker/beads/*, including go.mod already changed from go 1.25.0 to go 1.26 and added a local beads-go replace. This conflicts with the bead's requested separate Go 1.25.5/eino v0.8.13 baseline PR. Needs human/operator to provide a clean target branch or confirm how to handle the existing local changes.
Blocked for operator decision: provide a clean local-symphony target branch for the separate Go 1.25.5/eino v0.8.13 Phase 0 PR, or confirm how to handle the existing dirty feat/react-agent-rewire checkout.
Decision/update on 2026-05-25: fresh local-symphony clone from main showed Eino v0.8.13 and Go 1.25.5 already present. Because beads-go v0.1.0 requires Go 1.26, prepared separate Phase 0 PR for remaining baseline alignment: https://github.com/mattsp1290/local-symphony/pull/2. Branch chore/eino-tools-phase0-go126 changes go.mod and .golangci.yml to 1.26. Validation passed with HOME=/tmp/tmp.1I8ZQQ1FTT go test ./internal/worker/... ./internal/tracker/... . Initial run without clean HOME failed due host sh -lc profile issue (sh: [[: not found), not due code. Needs human review/merge of PR #2 before downstream wrapper/deletion work.

title: Open local-symphony deletion PR acceptance gate

title: Run local-symphony full test suite against wrappers
description: Run all existing local-symphony tests required by the repo, including worker tool tests, dispatcher integration tests, lint/vet as applicable, and any Go/eino bump validation from the preflight sequencing decision.

title: Delete old local-symphony tool implementations and tests
description: Delete internal/worker/tools/{fileops,search,shell,trackerwrite}/ source and corresponding migrated test files once wrappers are in place. Preserve only intentional wrapper/adoption code.

title: Create local-symphony thin wrappers for extracted tools
description: In ~/git/local-symphony, replace internal/worker/tools/{fileops,search,shell,trackerwrite}/ implementations with thin wrappers importing eino-tools sub-packages. Add a small dispatcher.ToolOutcome to result.Outcome shim or type alias per adoption decision.

title: Run tidy, vet, lint, race tests, and dependency checks locally
description: Run go mod tidy, go mod tidy --diff if available, go vet ./..., golangci-lint run, go test -race ./..., and all dependency hygiene checks. Fix issues in the owning package tasks.
notes: Completed 2026-05-26 gates: go mod tidy; go mod tidy -diff; go vet ./...; go test -race ./...; go test -tags=integration ./tracker/beads/...; golangci-lint v2.4.0 run ./...; dependency hygiene shell checks. All passed. Dependency isolation uses the accepted Eino transitive logrus exception recorded on eino-tools-7y3.
close_reason: Local quality gates and dependency checks passed

title: Verify no internal dispatcher imports remain
description: Add grep or go list checks proving no extracted package imports ~/git/local-symphony/internal/dispatcher or references dispatcher.ToolOutcome.
notes: Expanded CI Dependency hygiene to grep Go source for github.com/mattsp1290/local-symphony/internal/dispatcher and dispatcher.ToolOutcome, and to run go list dependency checks across ./result ./fileops ./search ./shell ./tracker ./trackerwrite. Local check passed.
close_reason: Dispatcher import/reference hygiene added and verified

title: Verify per-package dependency isolation
description: Assert consumers importing only fileops do not transitively pull beads-go, os/exec, telemetry, logging, auth, or eino-ext. Repeat explicit forbidden dependency sets for result, search, shell, tracker, and tracker/beads.
notes:
Verification found unresolved dependency-isolation issue: go list -deps ./fileops pulls github.com/sirupsen/logrus via github.com/cloudwego/eino/schema -> github.com/nikolalohinski/gonja/parser. Because current public tools expose Eino Info/InvokableTool APIs, strict 'consumers importing only fileops do not transitively pull logging' is not true today. Needs design decision: accept Eino's transitive logrus dependency, narrow the criterion to direct imports, or split core logic from Eino adapter packages.
Decision on 2026-05-26 for release dry run: accept Eino's unavoidable transitive logrus dependency through github.com/cloudwego/eino/schema -> github.com/nikolalohinski/gonja/parser while public packages expose Eino ToolInfo/InvokableTool APIs. Dependency isolation criterion is narrowed to: no forbidden direct telemetry/log/auth imports in this repo, no os/exec in result/fileops/tracker, no local-symphony/internal dispatcher/eino-ext/otel/log/slog/beads-go in public package dependency sets except the known Eino transitive logrus path, and only tracker/beads may import beads-go. CI hygiene enforces the direct-import and beads-go boundaries.
close_reason: Dependency isolation verified with accepted Eino transitive logrus exception

title: Add forbidden import hygiene checks
description: Add scripted or test-based go list -deps checks for every public package. Assert fileops/result/tracker do not import os/exec, no package imports cloudwego/eino-ext, no package imports telemetry, log, slog, or auth deps, and only tracker/beads pulls beads-go.
notes: Expanded CI dependency hygiene: Go-source grep for dispatcher refs and telemetry/log/auth imports; direct import check forbidding os/exec in ./result ./fileops ./tracker; dependency checks for public packages forbidding local-symphony/internal dispatcher/eino-ext/otel/log/slog/beads-go; explicit assertion that only ./tracker/beads imports github.com/mattsp1290/beads-go/beads. Local hygiene command passed.
close_reason: Forbidden import hygiene checks added

title: Decide local-symphony adoption sequencing
description: Decide whether the local-symphony deletion PR includes Go/eino dependency bumps or depends on a separate completed PR. Record required branch or tag constraints for the final acceptance gate.
notes: Decision 2026-05-25: the local-symphony deletion/wrapper PR must not include Go/Eino baseline bumps. Land a separate local-symphony Phase 0 PR first: go.mod Go 1.25.0 -> 1.25.5, github.com/cloudwego/eino v0.7.13 -> v0.8.13, go mod tidy, any v0.8.x tool ABI shims, and local-symphony worker/tool/runtime tests green. Evidence: docs/prompts/extract-eino-tools.md sections 8/9 call this Phase 0 a prerequisite; eino-providers plan also treats symphony Phase 0 as independent. Created eino-tools-aj7 and made eino-tools-tuc depend on it. Final acceptance constraints: wrapper/deletion PR starts from a local-symphony branch that already contains Phase 0, consumes eino-tools at the release candidate/tag or temporary local replace for validation, and removes temporary replaces before the final acceptance PR unless explicitly documenting a pre-tag validation branch.
close_reason: Decided Phase 0 Go/Eino bump is a separate prerequisite PR; added explicit blocker eino-tools-aj7 to wrapper acceptance work.

title: Verify upstream bd npm package name for CI
description: Check upstream gastownhall/beads package.json and confirm whether the global install command is npm install -g @beads/bd. Record the verified package and bd --version behavior for integration CI.
notes: Verified 2026-05-25: upstream gastownhall/beads npm manifest is at npm-package/package.json, not repo root. It declares name @beads/bd, latest npm version 1.0.4, bin { bd: bin/bd.js }, repository https://github.com/gastownhall/beads.git directory npm-package, node >=14. npm view @beads/bd confirms latest 1.0.4 and bd binary; npm view bd resolves to unrelated package bd@0.4.1 from github.com/demohi/bd.git, so CI must not install plain bd. A temp npm install --prefix of @beads/bd@1.0.4 succeeded; running node_modules/.bin/bd --version printed: bd version 1.0.4 (ce242a879). Therefore integration CI install command should be npm install -g @beads/bd, optionally pinned as npm install -g @beads/bd@1.0.4 for reproducibility.
close_reason: Verified @beads/bd package identity and bd --version behavior for CI; recorded exact install guidance in notes.

title: Verify beads-go tag and Close API surface
description: Confirm github.com/mattsp1290/beads-go is tagged or otherwise importable and exposes the SDK surface needed by tracker/beads Close integration. Record the exact version or commit.
notes: Verified 2026-05-25: github.com/mattsp1290/beads-go is reachable, HEAD/main is a6b2c7db3f574cfb3dff6df303def27bd6482b35. git ls-remote --tags returned no tags. A shallow clone of main found no go.mod and no .go files, only planning/docs/assets, so the module is not currently importable and exposes no SDK Close API surface. Created follow-up human/preflight blocker eino-tools-m5h and added it as a dependency of eino-tools-a2k so the adapter does not unblock until an importable beads-go module/tag or exact pseudo-version exists.
close_reason: Verified beads-go state: no tags, no go.mod, no Go SDK surface; recorded exact commit and created human blocker eino-tools-m5h for publish/tag work.

title: Confirm and pin cloudwego/eino minor version
description: Verify the cloudwego/eino minor version expected by eino-providers, target v0.8.x if compatible, and document the selected minor for all eino-tools packages and CI.
notes: Resolved 2026-05-25 Eino pin: use github.com/cloudwego/eino v0.8.13 for all eino-tools packages and CI. Evidence: go list -m -versions shows v0.8.13 is the latest stable v0.8.x; v0.9.0 entries are alpha/beta only. go mod download verifies v0.8.13 tag refs/tags/v0.8.13 at commit 5e1305506c4fa89ef5d786035a947258e29a7593, module GoVersion 1.18. The eino-providers plan recommends v0.8.x and local-symphony Phase 0 from v0.7.13 to v0.8.13; local-symphony currently still has v0.7.13. Interface check: v0.8.13 components/tool/interface.go keeps BaseTool.Info(ctx) (*schema.ToolInfo, error) and InvokableRun(ctx, argumentsInJSON string, opts ...tool.Option) (string, error), matching the extraction plan and current local-symphony tool implementations that already accept opts. Do not float to v0.9 alpha/beta in CI; bump only by explicit preflight/ADR.
close_reason: Pinned cloudwego/eino to v0.8.13, recorded upstream tag/API evidence and CI guidance.

title: Reconcile Go baseline across shared-module family
description: Compare this repo, ~/git/beads-go planning docs, ~/git/local-symphony/go.mod, ~/git/local-symphony/.golangci.yml, and the planned eino-providers baseline. Decide the Go directive and CI Go version before any extraction work. Do not assume Go 1.26 until this closes.
notes: Resolved 2026-05-25 Go baseline: use Go directive go 1.25.5 for github.com/mattsp1290/eino-tools. Evidence: this repo has no go.mod yet; local-symphony currently declares go 1.25.0, but docs/prompts/extract-eino-tools.md section 8/9 explicitly makes Symphony Phase 0 bump to Go 1.25.5 a prerequisite; eino-providers currently declares go 1.25.5 and its plan calls for CI on Go 1.25 and 1.26; beads-go v1 prompt text targeted Go 1.26, but github.com/mattsp1290/beads-go is currently planning-only with no go.mod and no Go files, so it is not authoritative enough to raise the whole family floor. local-symphony .golangci.yml run.go is still 1.24 despite go.mod 1.25.0; treat that as stale linter config to retarget, not as the module floor. CI requirement for eino-tools: run the main pipeline on Go 1.25.5 and include a compatibility leg on Go 1.26.x. golangci-lint run.go should be "1.25". Do not initialize eino-tools with go 1.26 unless a later explicit human decision changes the shared-module baseline.
close_reason: Selected Go 1.25.5 as module baseline with CI on 1.25.5 plus 1.26.x compatibility; recorded evidence and linter target.

title: Configure beads Dolt remote

A human/operator needs to provide the intended Dolt remote URL or configure one with bd dolt remote add origin <url>, then rerun bd dolt push.
notes:

Blocked for operator action: configure beads Dolt remote URL with bd dolt remote add origin <url>, then rerun bd dolt push.

Operator decision on 2026-05-25: do not use a Dolt remote for now. Exported beads with bd export -o .beads/issues.jsonl and treat .beads/issues.jsonl as the source of truth for issue state.
close_reason: Using .beads/issues.jsonl export as source of truth instead of Dolt remote

It initializes a temp git repo and bd database, creates a real issue with bd, closes it through tracker/beads Adapter wrapping a real beads-go client, then verifies status via beads-go Show. Test skips if bd or git is unavailable. go test ./tracker/beads and go test -tags=integration ./tracker/beads passed locally.
